channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Sonos outlines a plan to win back user trust after its app fiasco
item.description: Speaker company Sonos laid out a number of new plans and commitments on Tuesday in an attempt to turn around the beleaguered sound company following a bug-riddled app relaunch in May that’s still impacting the company.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: 2024 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: New Jersey offshore wind farm clears big federal hurdle amid environmental concerns
item.description: SEA GIRT, N.J. (AP) — The federal government gave a key approval Tuesday to an offshore wind farm in New Jersey, even as residents in the town where its power cable would come ashore worry it could go through underground toxic waste that's still being cleaned up. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: WAYNE PARRY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Australia's online dating industry adopts code of conduct to keep users safer
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — A code of conduct will be enforced on the online dating industry to better protect Australian users after research found that three-in-four people suffer some form of sexual violence through the platforms, Australia’s government said on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Two good Visual Studio Code alternatives
item.description: The conductor of my choir famously tells us singers, “I only want everything all the time.” Well, as a developer, my mantra for code editors and IDEs (integrated development environments) is exactly that: I only want everything all the time.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: What to stream: Coldplay, an unhinged Charlie Puth, 'Salem’s Lot,' Sarah Paulson and NHL 25
item.description: Coldplay's 10th studio album and the American Music Awards celebrating their 50th anniversary are some of the new television, films, music and games headed to a device near you.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Helene and other storms dumped a whopping 40 trillion gallons of rain on the South
item.description: Meteorologists calculate that more than 40 trillion gallons of rain drenched the Southeast United States in the last week from Hurricane Helene and a run-of-the-mill rainstorm that sloshed in ahead of it.
item.dc:creator: Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Waymo self-driving car interrupts Vice President Harris’ motorcade in San Francisco
item.description: Harris, the Democratic presidential nominee, was in the city staying overnight Friday at the Fairmont Hotel before a campaign fundraiser at the San Francisco Palace of Fine Arts Saturday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Kristin J. Bender, The Mercury News

item.title: Arkansas sues YouTube over claims that the site is fueling a mental health crisis
item.description: Arkansas has sued YouTube and its parent company Alphabet, saying the video-sharing platform is deliberately addictive and fueling a mental health crisis among youth.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Demillo, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Verizon Cell Service Restored After Massive Outage
item.description: Verizon cell service restored after outage affecting customers in the US. Cause and scope of disruption being investigated by Federal Communications Commission.
item.dc:creator: CNN.com Wire Service, The Mercury News

item.title: X's value has fallen 79% since Musk takeover, Fidelity estimates
item.description: Fidelity, which originally invested $19.66 million, now values its stake in the company at $4.19 million.
item.dc:creator: Katie Smith, NewsNation

item.title: 6 ways to apply automation in devsecops
item.description: The goal of devsecops is to integrate security throughout the software development life cycle. To do this, the security team can’t focus on just the most obvious software development vulnerabilities or just the processes that are the easiest to secure.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Newsom vetoes bill for stricter AI regulations
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom (D) on Sunday vetoed a landmark artificial intelligence (AI) bill that would have created new safety rules for the emerging tech, handing much of Silicon Valley a major win.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: California governor vetoes bill to create first-in-nation AI safety measures
item.description: The decision is a blow to efforts attempting to rein in the industry that is rapidly evolving with little oversight.
item.dc:creator: TRÂN NGUYỄN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: X requests it be reinstated in Brazil after complying with judge's orders, sources say
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — In the high-stakes showdown between the world’s richest man and a Brazilian Supreme Court justice, Elon Musk blinked. Musk’s social media site X has complied with Alexandre de Moraes’ orders and requested its service be reestablished in the country, two sources said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Observe unveils AI-powered agents to speed troubleshooting
item.description: Cloud observability provider Observe today introduced updates to its product set with Project Voyager, a SaaS offering that now includes an artificial intelligence (AI) investigating tool and capabilities to monitor application performance as well as to query Snowflake data storage services.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: One Tech Tip: Using Instagram's new teen account settings to supervise young users
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Worried about your teen's Instagram use? Faced with growing pressure to do more to stop online harm, the social media platform has rolled out a major new update to protect young people and give parents more control.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: OpenAI Chief Technology Officer Mira Murati and 2 other execs are leaving the ChatGPT maker
item.description: A high-ranking executive at OpenAI who served a few days as its interim CEO during a period of turmoil last year said she's leaving the artificial intelligence company.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Catch a ghost on your Ring camera? You can get $100K for the video
item.description: Ring is inviting users to submit videos showcasing paranormal activity for the chance to win a $100,000 prize.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, NewsNation

item.title: CrowdStrike executive apologizes to Congress for July global tech outage
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An executive at cybersecurity company CrowdStrike apologized in testimony to Congress for sparking a global technology outage over the summer. “We let our customers down,” said Adam Meyers, who leads CrowdStrike's threat intelligence division, in a hearing before a U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Age of AI 'Superintelligence'
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Can AI make video games more immersive? Some studios turn to AI-fueled NPCs for more interaction
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — For decades, video games have relied on scripted, stilted interactions with non-player characters to help shepherd gamers in their journeys.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Tearful Caroline Ellison gets 2 years in prison over her role in FTX fraud
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Caroline Ellison, a former top executive in Sam Bankman-Fried ’s fallen FTX cryptocurrency empire, was sentenced to two years in prison on Tuesday after she apologized to everyone hurt by a fraud that stole billions of dollars from investors, lenders and customers.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KRON4
